about.title: About
about.intro: NLP Engineer and M.Sc. Data Science student with experience in LLM fine-tuning, RAG systems, automated text analysis, and full-stack development.
body: I build scalable AI solutions that combine language models with real-world data — from resume ranking and automated annotation to RAG-powered search across internal databases.

At Devster Labs, I develop NLP systems that automate candidate pre-screening, fine-tune LLMs for domain-specific tasks, and integrate models into production workflows. Previously at ConsoleDot, I led full-stack development of AI-powered web applications using Next.js and generative AI.
I am currently pursuing a Master's in Data Science at Philipps-Universität Marburg, with coursework in Natural Language Processing, Machine Learning, Deep Learning, and Big Data Analytics.

posts.title: Designing RAG Systems for Internal Knowledge Search
posts.excerpt: How retrieval-augmented generation connects language models to internal databases for targeted, context-aware search.
body: Retrieval-augmented generation bridges the gap between general-purpose language models and organization-specific knowledge. Instead of relying on the model's training data alone, a RAG pipeline retrieves relevant documents from internal sources and grounds the model's responses in that context.

The key engineering decisions sit in the retrieval layer: chunking strategy, embedding model selection, vector store design, and how aggressively to filter results before passing them to the LLM. Each choice affects both answer quality and latency.

In production systems — such as candidate search across internal databases — the goal is not just accurate answers but also traceability: users need to know which sources informed a response, and the system must scale as the underlying data grows.

posts.title: Fine-Tuning LLMs for Domain-Specific Annotation
posts.excerpt: Practical lessons from fine-tuning a large language model for automatic audio-data annotation, reaching 92% accuracy.
body: General-purpose language models handle broad tasks well, but domain-specific annotation — such as labeling audio-derived text data — often requires fine-tuning on curated examples that reflect the target distribution.

The pipeline starts with data quality: representative samples, consistent labeling guidelines, and enough volume to cover edge cases. Fine-tuning then adapts the model's behavior without the cost and latency of prompting a base model on every example.

Reaching high accuracy on annotation tasks is less about model size and more about alignment between training data and production inputs. Monitoring drift, retraining on new error patterns, and maintaining a human review loop keep the system reliable over time.

projects.title: NLP Resume Ranking System
projects.description: An NLP-based resume ranking system that automates candidate pre-screening, achieving 90% efficiency gains.
projects.details: Built at Devster Labs to replace manual resume review with automated scoring and ranking based on job requirements and candidate qualifications.
body: This system applies natural language processing to parse, analyze, and rank candidate resumes against open positions — reducing the time recruiters spend on initial screening.

The pipeline handles document ingestion, text extraction, semantic matching against job descriptions, and ranked output for hiring teams. Efficiency improvements reached 90% compared to manual pre-screening.
The project demonstrates how targeted NLP automation can deliver immediate operational value in recruitment workflows.

projects.title: LLM Fine-Tuning for Audio Annotation
projects.description: Fine-tuned a large language model for automatic audio-data annotation, reaching 92% accuracy.
projects.details: Developed at Devster Labs to automate the labeling of audio-derived text data using a domain-adapted language model.
body: Manual annotation of audio data is slow and inconsistent at scale. This project fine-tunes an LLM on curated annotation examples to produce reliable labels automatically.
The workflow covers data preparation, model selection, fine-tuning configuration, evaluation against a held-out test set, and deployment into the annotation pipeline.
The fine-tuned model achieved 92% accuracy, significantly reducing manual review while maintaining quality through periodic human oversight.

projects.title: RAG Candidate Search Chatbot
projects.description: A RAG-powered chatbot for targeted candidate searches across internal databases.
projects.details: Designed and developed at Devster Labs to let recruiters query candidate records in natural language with responses grounded in internal data.
body: The chatbot combines retrieval-augmented generation with Devster Labs' internal candidate database, enabling natural-language queries like "find candidates with NLP experience in Berlin."
The architecture includes document embedding, vector search, context assembly, and LLM response generation — integrated into a scalable backend service.
By grounding answers in actual database records, the system avoids hallucination while making internal talent data accessible through conversational search.

projects.title: ConsoleDot AI Platform
projects.description: A collaborative platform for creatives using Next.js and generative AI to create NFTs, plus a dating app with AI-powered auto-suggestions.
projects.details: Built at ConsoleDot, where I led technical development of full-stack AI-powered web applications.
body: At ConsoleDot, I developed a collaborative creative platform that uses generative AI and Next.js to help users produce and mint NFTs — combining modern frontend patterns with AI-generated content workflows.
I also led the technical development of a dating application featuring an AI-powered auto-suggestion function that recommends conversation starters and profile improvements based on user context.
Both projects required full-stack ownership: API design, frontend implementation, AI integration, and team coordination across the development lifecycle.
